concepts.title: Dot Product of Two Vectors
concepts.description: The dot product of two vectors a and b is given by a · b = |a| |b| cos θ, where |a| and |b| are the magnitudes of the vectors and θ is the angle between them.

concepts.title: Scalar Triple Product
concepts.description: The scalar triple product of three vectors a, b, and c is given by [a b c] = a · (b × c). It is used to find the volume of a parallelepiped.

concepts.title: Cross Product of Two Vectors
concepts.description: The cross product of two vectors a and b is given by a × b = |a| |b| sin θ n, where n is the unit vector perpendicular to both a and b, and θ is the angle between them.

concepts.title: Right-Hand Rule for Cross Product
concepts.description: To find the direction of the cross product of two vectors, use the right-hand rule: point your thumb in the direction of the first vector and your fingers in the direction of the second vector. Your palm will face the direction of the cross product.

concepts.title: Mistake in Finding the Magnitude of a Vector
concepts.description: Students often forget to take the square root when finding the magnitude of a vector. Remember to take the square root of the sum of the squares of the components of the vector.

concepts.title: Unit Vectors
concepts.description: A unit vector is a vector with a magnitude of 1. It is used to represent the direction of a vector. The unit vectors in the x, y, and z directions are i, j, and k respectively.

concepts.title: Projection of a Vector
concepts.description: The projection of a vector a onto a vector b is given by proj_b(a) = (a · b / |b|^2) b. It is used to find the component of a vector in the direction of another vector.

concepts.title: Vector Equation of a Line
concepts.description: The vector equation of a line passing through a point a and parallel to a vector b is given by r = a + λb, where λ is a scalar parameter.
